# chronosphere_gcp_metrics_integration (Resource)

Scrapes Google Cloud Monitoring metrics from the configured projects and metric prefixes using a GCP service-account credential, with optional filters and rollup rules.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "chronosphere_gcp_metrics_integration" "customer" {
  name = "GCP Metrics Integration"
  slug = "gcp-metrics-integration"
  service_account {
    client_email = "chronosphere-collector@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the GCP metrics integration.

### Optional

- `metric_groups` (Block List) Groups of Google Cloud metrics to ingest. Each group targets a specific project and set of metric prefixes. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metric_groups))
- `service_account` (Block List, Max: 1) Google Cloud service account that Chronosphere impersonates to read metrics. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--service_account))
- `slug` (String) Stable identifier for the integration. Generated from `name` if omitted. Immutable after creation.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--metric_groups"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metric_groups`

Required:

- `project_id` (String) Google Cloud project ID to read metrics from. The configured service account must have access.

Optional:

- `filters` (Block List) Label filters applied to metrics in this group. All filters must match for a metric to be ingested. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metric_groups--filters))
- `prefixes` (List of String) List of Google Cloud metric prefixes to ingest (e.g. `compute.googleapis.com/`).
- `rollup_rules` (Block List) Server-side aggregation rules applied to metrics in this group before they are stored. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metric_groups--rollup_rules))

<a id="nestedblock--metric_groups--filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metric_groups.filters`

Optional:

- `context` (String) Label context, e.g. resource vs. metric label. See the Chronosphere GCP integration documentation for accepted values.
- `name` (String) Label name to filter on.
- `value_glob` (String) Value pattern using glob syntax (e.g. `prod-*`). An exact match is applied when no glob characters are present.


<a id="nestedblock--metric_groups--rollup_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metric_groups.rollup_rules`

Optional:

- `aggregation` (String) Aggregation function applied across the dropped labels (e.g. sum, max).
- `label_policy` (Block List, Max: 1) Specifies which labels to preserve during aggregation. Labels not listed are dropped. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metric_groups--rollup_rules--label_policy))
- `metric_name` (String) Fully-qualified Google Cloud metric name the rollup rule targets (e.g. `cloudsql.googleapis.com/database/uptime`).

<a id="nestedblock--metric_groups--rollup_rules--label_policy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metric_groups.rollup_rules.label_policy`

Optional:

- `keep` (Block List) Labels to retain after aggregation. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--metric_groups--rollup_rules--label_policy--keep))

<a id="nestedblock--metric_groups--rollup_rules--label_policy--keep"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metric_groups.rollup_rules.label_policy.keep`

Optional:

- `context` (String) Label context, e.g. resource vs. metric label.
- `name` (String) Name of the label to keep.





<a id="nestedblock--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_account`

Required:

- `client_email` (String) Email address of the Google Cloud service account to impersonate for authentication.
